title: Bridge Essay: Psychedelics disrupt hierarchical cortical propagations in the default mode network of humans and mice.
concept: Nonordinary states as controlled experiments in consciousness — predictive processing breakdown and revision.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nonordinary Rendering** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nonordinary states as controlled experiments in consciousness — predictive processing breakdown and revision.

**Psychedelics disrupt hierarchical cortical propagations in the default mode network of humans and mice.** - Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2026). DOI: 10.1073/pnas.2522000123. PMID: 42258710. Psychedelic drugs are poised to become mainstream treatments, yet we lack a circuit-level account of how they reshape brain activity. Emerging evidence suggests that multiple psychedelic compounds modulate activity in the brain's default mode network (DMN), often interpreted as either increased or decreased bottom-up hierarchical processing.
